perturbation.original_text: The following terms are defined as follows: (a) "Evaluation Material" means any oral or written information concerning Sunglass Hut, its subsidiaries, business, markets, products, prospects or finances furnished by it (in such capacity, the "Provider") or any of its Representatives (as defined below) to Luxottica (in such capacity, the "Recipient") or any of its Representatives, except information which: (i) is or becomes generally available to or known by the public (other than as a result of a disclosure directly or indirectly by the Recipient or any of its Representatives who received such information pursuant hereto); (ii) is or becomes available to the Recipient or any of its Representatives on a nonconfidential basis from a source other than the Provider or any of its Representatives; provided, that such source is not and was not bound by a confidentiality agreement with, or other similar obligation to, the Provider of which the Recipient or any of its Representatives are aware; or (iii) has been independently acquired or developed by the Recipient or any of its Representatives without violating any of its obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The following terms are defined as follows: (a) "Evaluation Material" means information. This definition is intentionally vague.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Evaluation Material' is replaced with a vague statement. Later references to 'Evaluation Material' throughout the document now lack clear meaning, creating uncertainty in enforcement. Specifically, the deletion of the details regarding exceptions (i, ii, iii) to what constitutes 'Evaluation Material' creates confusion.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any Evaluation Material provided to the Recipient hereunder will be used by the Recipient and its Representatives solely for the purpose of evaluating the Possible Transaction and will not be used by the Recipient in any way detrimental to the Provider or its stockholders. The Recipient will maintain the strict confidentiality of any Evaluation Material provided to it or any of its Representatives by the other Party or any of the other Party's Representatives and will not disclose any part of it to any other person; provided, however, that it may disclose any such Evaluation Material or portions thereof to those of its Representatives who need to know such information in order to assist the Recipient in evaluating the advisability of entering into the Possible Transaction and it may disclose any such Evaluation Material in accordance with Paragraph 5 hereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces a detailed definition of 'Evaluation Material' with a vague one ('information'). The contradicted text refers to 'Evaluation Material' and imposes obligations on its use and confidentiality. Because the definition has been made intentionally vague, it is now unclear what constitutes 'Evaluation Material', thus contradicting the section that relies on a clear understanding of the term. This makes it impossible to determine the scope of the confidentiality obligation, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (b) A "Representative" of a Party includes any officer, director, shareholder, employee, advisor, attorney, consultant or agent of such Party.
perturbation.changed_text: (b) A "Representative" of a Party includes any officer or director of such Party.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of "Representative" is narrowed, excluding advisors, attorneys, consultants or agents. This creates a contradiction because Section 3 and 4 refers to the original definition, and now that has been changed, it will contradict the mentioned sections.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient will (a) provide the other Party with a list of its Representatives who are given access to any Evaluation Material, (b) inform each such Representative of the terms of this Agreement, and (c) obtain the agreement of each such Representative to abide by the terms of this Agreement. Each Party will be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed text narrows the definition of "Representative" by excluding advisors, attorneys, consultants, and agents. The contradicted text requires the Recipient to inform and obtain agreement from "each such Representative." Since the definition of Representative has changed, the obligation to inform and obtain agreement now applies to a smaller group, creating a direct conflict with the original intent of the contradicted text. The change fundamentally breaks the compatibility of the two sections because the responsibility for breaches by agents, advisors, attorneys, and consultants is no longer clearly assigned under the altered definition, creating a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party will indemnify, defend and hold harmless the other Party from and against any and all damages, losses, liabilities and costs that any other Party may sustain or incur as a result of any breach of this Agreement by such Party or any of its Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party will indemnify, defend and hold harmless the other Party from and against any and all damages that any other Party may sustain or incur as a result of any breach of this Agreement by such Party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'losses, liabilities and costs', the scope of indemnification is significantly reduced. This creates an in-text contradiction. While the remaining text still refers to indemnification, the scope of it is now much more limited. This creates a direct contradiction of what was previously stated regarding losses, liabilities, and costs.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties acknowledge that money damages are an inadequate remedy for breach of this Agreement because of the difficulty of ascertaining the amount of damage that will be suffered in the event that this Agreement is breached. Therefore, each Party will be entitled to equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance, in the event of any breach of the provisions of this Agreement by the other Party or any of its Representatives, in addition to all other remedies available to such Party at law or in equity.
perturbation.justification: The original text included 'losses, liabilities and costs' in the indemnification clause. The changed text removes 'losses, liabilities and costs', limiting the scope of indemnification to only 'damages'. The contradicted text states that money damages are an inadequate remedy for a breach. The changed text reduces the scope of the indemnity, making it potentially inadequate to cover all potential losses from a breach. The change introduces an in-text contradiction because the reduced indemnification might not adequately address the inadequacy of money damages as previously acknowledged, which may now conflict with the clause which calls for equitable relief.

perturbation.original_text: In such event, the Recipient promptly will return to the Provider all copies of any Evaluation Material in its possession or in the possession of any of its Representatives furnished by such Provider or any of its Representatives, and the Recipient will destroy all analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by it or any of its Representatives or for their use containing or reflecting any Evaluation Material, whether in digital, written or other form. Any destruction required pursuant to this Paragraph 6 will be certified in writing to the Provider by an authorized officer supervising such destruction.
perturbation.changed_text: In such event, the Recipient promptly will return to the Provider all copies of any Evaluation Material in its possession furnished by such Provider, and the Recipient will destroy all analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by it for their use containing or reflecting any Evaluation Material, whether in digital, written or other form. Any destruction required pursuant to this Paragraph 6 will be certified in writing to the Provider by an authorized officer supervising such destruction.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'or in the possession of any of its Representatives' is removed. The legal agreement now contradicts itself, as a breach of the agreement by the Representative isn't included, which can create problems in section 4, Indemnification.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party will indemnify, defend and hold harmless the other Party from and against any and all damages that any other Party may sustain or incur as a result of any breach of this Agreement by such Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes 'or in the possession of any of its Representatives'. This creates a contradiction with the 'Indemnification' section. The original text explicitly included the Representatives' actions, making the Recipient responsible for their breaches. The changed text removes this, meaning the Recipient isn't automatically responsible for the Representative's breach, potentially conflicting with the Indemnification clause, which states each party will indemnify the other for any breach. This constitutes a strong in-text contradiction because the obligation to indemnify is weakened by removing liability for actions of representatives.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party covenants that for a period of two (2) years following the date of this Agreement, such Party will not, directly or indirectly, solicit for employment or hire any employee of the other Party or any of the other Party's subsidiaries with whom such Party has had contact or who became known to such Party in connection with its consideration of the Possible Transaction; provided, however, that the foregoing provision will not prevent a Party from employing any employee of the other Party who contacts the prospective employer Party on his or her own initiative without any direct or indirect solicitation by or encouragement from the prospective employer Party, or prevent a general solicitation conducted in the ordinary course of business and not directed specifically at the employees of the other Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party covenants that for a period of two (2) years following the date of this Agreement, such Party will not, directly or indirectly, solicit for employment or hire any employee of the other Party or any of the other Party's subsidiaries with whom such Party has had contact or who became known to such Party in connection with its consideration of the Possible Transaction.
perturbation.explanation: The clause now does not include provisions for employees contacting potential employers on their own without any direct/indirect solicitation and general solicitation not specifically directed at the other party's employees. By removing the mentioned clause, a contradiction happens as now any sort of contact counts as a breach of the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party covenants that for a period of two (2) years following the date of this Agreement, such Party will not, directly or indirectly, solicit for employment or hire any employee of the other Party or any of the other Party's subsidiaries with whom such Party has had contact or who became known to such Party in connection with its consideration of the Possible Transaction.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the exceptions for unsolicited contact from employees and general solicitations, directly contradicting the intention in the original text to allow for those scenarios. By removing these clauses, the changed text creates a scenario where even passive contact could be construed as a breach, which clashes with the original intent. Furthermore, the contradicted text is exactly the same as the original text that was modified; therefore, they would be contradictory to one another.
